meaning: No practical pre-failure indication; failure may be sudden or hidden until system effect occurs.
Latent or internal damage with weak symptoms and limited inspection access.
Detection depends on targeted inspection or teardown; symptoms may arrive late.
Possible to detect, but not reliably with routine controls.
Default when evidence does not clearly show strong monitoring or hidden sudden failure.
Routine inspection, check, test, or replacement instruction improves detectability.
Clear inspection, borescope, oil debris, pressure, temperature, vibration, sensor, or alarm evidence.
Multiple direct monitoring or inspection indicators are present.
Continuous monitoring or direct alarm is expected to detect the condition before propagation.
Near-certain detection with direct controls before the failure effect occurs.
